race.comment: PINITO (2) beat a number of today's rivals when she took out a Group 3 over this distance at Doomben last start. That was her first run in six weeks so she should improve further. Perfectly drawn so there is no reason she cannot get the job done again. SURFIN' BIRD (5) just missed first-up in the G3 Hawkesbury Crown then went around as favourite and looked to have every chance when fifth to Tuileries in the Dark Jewel at Scone three weeks ago. Unbeaten at the mile so deserves another chance despite the wide alley. DECLICHY BOULEVARD (9) came from a minute back to record a most impressive win first-up from a 60-week break over 1400m at Randwick recently. Group 1 placed as a three-year-old. Has the class to give this field a fright. PIPPIE BEACH (10) overcame an awkward start to flash home for a close third to PINITO last start. Ready to peak at the third run back from a spell. OUR QUEEN (11) was fourth in the PINITO race to add to a string of solid performances. Must go in exotics.
